human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for apparel sample-room specificity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, traceability and hold-boundary handling, commercialization completeness, and validated spec-pack consistency.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to apparel sample-room execution, including request intake, reservation, pull control, custody tracking, courier handoffs, due-date and return handling, blocked-work escalation, and completion evidence.

Authority sources cover GS1 and GS1 US apparel identity, attributes, package-measurement, floor-ready, and raw-material guidance, plus FTC, CPSC tracking-label and recall guidance, and ISO nonconformity boundaries.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing intake prerequisites, missing samples, conflicting pulls, traceability conflicts, quality holds, unsupported substitution requests, technical-approval rejection, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, tenant adaptation, and compliance-boundary rejection.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/apparel-textiles-and-footwear/sample-room-specialist/ so the overlay remains distinct from adjacent quality, supplier-quality, inventory, logistics, and technical-approval lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final fit approval, technical signoff, supplier approval, quality release, and regulatory or legal labeling authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated apparel sample-room specialist for request intake, sample reservation, custody control, shipping and returns, blocked-work handling, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
